observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant (MA) to identify the difference of P489,383.86 as of December 31,
2018 and to immediately prepare the journal entries to record all valid
reconciling items. Henceforth, observe the proper preparation of the Bank
Reconciliation Statements, to attain fair presentation of the Cash in Bank
account in the Financial Statements.

2.13 We recommended that the LCE direct the:
a. Municipal Economic Enterprise Officer to strictly enforce the lease
contract agreement and require the collection of unpaid rentals. The
contract of lease should provide stiffer penalties in case of non-payment or
late payment of lease rental that will encourage stallholders to pay in due
time;
b. Municipal Treasurer (MT) to exert extra effort in the collection of stall
rentals and furnish the accounting office the report of rental receivables;
and
c. MA to recognize the receivable and the corresponding income from
uncollected stall rentals.

3.9 We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to recognize expense on
Inventory accounts on the month when the goods are consumed or distributed
based on a monthly summary of supplies and materials issued supported with
requisition and issued slips, prepared by the supply officer.

4.8 We recommended that the MT deposit all collections intact or where collections
are minimal and daily deposits thereof becomes costly and impractical, the
collections shall be deposited at least once a week, or as soon as the collections
reach P10,000.00 to prevent possible loss or misuse of the fund.

5.7 We recommended that the LCE investigate the cause of delay in the payment of
the loan amortizations, and require the persons responsible thereof to refund the
amount of P93,262.87 as penalty imposed by MDFO. Henceforth, ensure that all
obligations are paid on time to avoid penalties and surcharges.

6.11 We recommended that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
and Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit the lacking contract documents
and henceforth, adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its
RIRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the
conduct of a strict evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the
submission of complete contract documents.

7.9 We recommended that the LCE require the BAC and Municipal Accountant to
oblige the concerned suppliers and contractors to post warranty securities for the
procurement of good and infrastructure projects that had already been fully
paid. Henceforth, strictly observe the mandatory posting of warranty security to
guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities pursuant to
Section 62 of the IRR of RA 9184 and Section 9.1.1.4 and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

8.5 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineering Office /
General Service Office to submit the approved suspension and resumption
order, if any, or an explanation for the delay in delivery of goods, supplies and
materials or project completion, otherwise collect the corresponding amount of
liquidated damages from the supplier/contractor. Likewise, require the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that only claims with complete supporting
documents are processed and paid.

9.9 We recommended that the LCE require:
a) the LDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and MA to reconcile their
records and revise the report on the utilization of the CY 2018 LDRRMF to
reflect the correct charging of expenditures to the programs, projects and
activities included in the LDRRMFIP and to arrive at the correct
unexpended balance due for transfer to the Trust Fund;
b) the MA to submit the lacking disbursement voucher amounting to
P15,249.06, and the relevant documents to support the issuance to the end
users of the personal protective equipment costing P183,366.00, otherwise
reclassify the same to appropriate inventory account; and maintain
subsidiary records to monitor LDRRMF in the Trust Fund; and
c) henceforth, ensure that the mandatory appropriation equivalent to five
percent (5%) of the total estimated revenues are complied with, and
include/consider the LDRRMF in the Trust Fund in the preparation of
Annual LDRRMFIP in the prescribed form, to avoid idle funds and
maximize disaster preparedness and response programs.

9.10 We also recommended to look into the cause of imposition of penalties of
P28,740.00 by the National Telecommunications Commission, and require
responsible personnel to refund the same.

10.9 We recommended that the LCE require the MA to verify the balance of the Due
to BIR account as of December 31, 2018 and the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately remit the full amount. Henceforth, oblige the MT to maintain
subsidiary ledgers for each tax type and all others concerned in the withholding
and remittance of taxes to strictly comply with the related BIR regulations.

11.7 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to verify the
balances of the accounts as of December 31, 2018 and the Municipal Treasurer
to immediately remit the full amount. Henceforth, strictly remit all withheld
amount to the agencies concerned before due date.

We recommended that (1) all revenue collectors be required to turn over the full amount of their collections at the close of business day to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to (2) conduct verification of collections and accountable forms, pursuant to Section 307 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 60 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the receivable accounts with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, and (4) effect adjustments to correct the balances thereof. Henceforth, (5) ensure that receivable accounts are valued at the actual amount of tax due and collectible, to attain fair presentation in the Financial Statements.

observations.managementAction: require the collector to render an accounting and to turnover the corresponding amounts collected; post announcement for taxpayers to present temporary receipts; file appropriate charges when warranted.
observations.reason: Turnover and reporting of temporary receipt collections remained outstanding; corrective actions pending.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant must submit on time the accounts and financial reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: submit on time the accounts and financial reports of the LGU.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of accounts and financial reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local
road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be substantially complied, thus,
require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads
and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.
Likewise, enjoin all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule
for the computation of depreciation, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road
Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, render a Report on
Local Road Network and provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial
Statements of the LGU.

We recommend that management exert effort to enforce collection of the
outstanding dormant receivables. However, for those found no longer collectible,
initiate the preparation and filing of the request for authority from the
Commission on Audit to write-off the accounts in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to transfer the
remaining unutilized LDRRMF to the Trust Fund in accordance with RA 10121
and Sections 4.4 and 5.1.13 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the LGU fast track the construction of a sanitary
landfill to replace the controlled dump site, design the MRFs according to
standard, develop the specific mechanics and guidelines for the implementation of
the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan, set up and adequate performance
monitoring system, and implement a “no segregation/no collection” policy as
prescribed in RA 9003.

We recommend that procurement of FOL products shall be covered by an
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as required in Section 7.2 of RA 9184. Likewise,
require concerned officers and employees to prepare and submit a Monthly
Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and properly
filled up Driver’s Trip Tickets. Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements
of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to control and
regulate the use of government vehicles.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to record the
purchases of drugs and medicines through the inventory account and to submit
the necessary documents to support the recognition of inventories as expense
amounting to ₱134,050.85. Likewise, the BAC is advised to require the suppliers
to submit Certificate(s) of Product Registration from the Food and Drugs
Administration (FDA) on the drugs and medicines procured and to obtain
certification from the Municipal Health Office that the medicines procured
conformed with the PNDF. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to
submit the disbursement vouchers covering the procurement of drugs and
medicines totaling ₱397,533.98.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor grant financial assistance to
other LGUs only when it is covered with a contract or Memorandum of
Agreement enumerating the conditions, requirements and responsibilities of the
recipients on the use of government funds. The grant of additional financial
assistance should be based on the recipient’s compliance with the provisions of the
MOA.

We recommend that henceforth the LGU submit a list of all its on-going
and up-coming projects/programs/activities (PPAs) at the beginning of the year
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. Likewise
submit a report on the posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public
notices within ten (10) days after the award of each infrastructure project or
before the start of any program or activity.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to limit the budget of Personal Services within 55% of the total
appropriation in accordance with Section 325 of RA 7160. Moreover, actual
disbursements for payroll should also be limited within the PS cap.

We recommend for the strict enforcement of the provisions stipulated in the
GAA relative to the minimum amount of P3,500.00 monthly net take home pay
of employees.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Inventory Committee to complete, within a specific timeframe, the conduct of physical count of all properties of LGU Calbiga and to prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment within one month from the completion of the inventory-taking. Previous audit recommendations on similar matter should also be studied to facilitate the procedures to be undertaken. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the RCPPE with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Inventory Committee to complete, within a specific timeframe, the conduct of physical count of all properties of LGU Calbiga and to prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment within one month from the completion of the inventory-taking.
observations.reason: Physical count and reports incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor: a.) Require the MDRRMO to revise the LDRRMIP in accordance with the prescribed formats and to include in the plan the activities to be funded from local funds only in order to present the accurate information on LDRRMF; b.) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to compute, record and report the accurate amount of allocation for LDRRMF prescribed by law and to retain in the books for the current year the unutilized amount for capital outlay only; Require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the remaining unutilized LDRRMF to the Trust Fund bank account.
observations.managementAction: Mayor: LDRRMF lacking.
observations.reason: Transfer of unexpended unutilized LDRRMF was implemented but amount transferred was insufficient; issue reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor submit the authority from the DAR approving the utilization of the project Equity in order to maximize the funds for its intended purposes.
observations.managementAction: submit the authority from the DAR approving the utilization of the project Equity.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned job order personnel to sign the contracts of service in order for it to be binding to both parties. Likewise, direct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to indicate in the contract of service the specific job description or work to be performed by a job order personnel.
observations.managementAction: require the concerned job order personnel to sign the contracts of service and direct the HRMO to indicate the specific job description or work to be performed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management liquidate all cash advances and strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: liquidate all cash advances and strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Collection Clerks must turn-over their collections intact at the close of each business day to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Collection Clerks must turn-over their collections intact at the close of each business day to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate guidelines in the implementation of this scholarship program. Moreover, applicants shall be thoroughly screened to ensure that only qualified students are admitted into the program. Require the submission of complete documents to support all grants of assistance under the program.
